mnemonics: P.A.M. (Pointers, Allocation, Management) - The three pillars of dynamic memory handling.
M.A.D. (Memory Allocation/Deallocation) - Always remember to deallocate what you allocate to avoid memory leaks.
Pointer Path (P* -> &x) - A pointer (P*) must hold the address (&x) of a variable to be useful.
keyPoints: Pointers are variables that store memory addresses, allowing indirect access to data.
Dynamic Memory Allocation (malloc, calloc) reserves memory at runtime, providing flexibility beyond fixed-size arrays.
Memory must be explicitly deallocated using free() to prevent memory leaks, as it is not automatically reclaimed.
commonMistakes: Dereferencing a NULL or uninitialized pointer, leading to segmentation faults.
Forgetting to free dynamically allocated memory, causing memory leaks that can consume all available RAM.
Using memory after it has been freed (dangling pointer), resulting in undefined and often catastrophic behavior.
